revision.comment: Text replacement - "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==" to "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->
{{distinguish|{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}}|{{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}|qq=}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Anashi-ni-imasu Hyōzu Shrine | image = File:Anashi-ni-imasu Hyōzu Shrine. Haiden(2017-11-19).jpg | coordinates = {{Coord|34.545|N|135.857778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 1065 Anashi, Sakurai, Nara Prefecture, Japan | deity = {{ill|Kushinadahime|qid=Q505469|1=Kushinadahime}}; {{ill|Ukanomitama|qid=Q3080728|1=Ukanomitama}} | type = {{nihongo|Shikinaisha|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=}} (Myōjin Taisha, Taisha, and Shōsha) | founded = Unknown | architecture = Nagare-zukuri (flowing style) | festivals = {{nihongo|April 8|4月8日|qq=}} and {{nihongo|October 21|10月21日|qq=}} | map = Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Sakurai|qid=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }} '''Anashi-ni-imasu Hyōzu Shrine''' ({{nihongo|穴師坐兵主神社|あなしにますひょうずじんじゃ|qq=}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|qid=Q127026|lt=Sakurai|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is listed among the {{ill|8=ja|9=Yamato Province の式内社一覧|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|lt=Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and was formerly classified as a [[Ken-sha|prefectural shrine]] ({{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=}}). The shrine was originally composed of three separate shrines: {{nihongo|Anashi-ni-imasu Hyōzu Shrine|穴師坐兵主神社|qq=}}, a Myōjin Taisha, {{nihongo|Makimuku-ni-imasu Wakumusubi Shrine|巻向坐若御魂神社|qq=}}, a Shikinaisha Taisha, and {{nihongo|Anashi-no-Ōhyozu Shrine|穴師大兵主神社|qq=}}, a Shikinaisha Shōsha. These were combined during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}} into the current site, which was originally the location of Anashi-no-Ōhyozu Shrine. == History == The origins of the shrine are unclear. According to one tradition, the original Anashi-ni-imasu Hyōzu Shrine was established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qid=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} in the 2nd year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}} to enshrine the guardian deity of the emperor’s meals. Another tradition attributes its founding to {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, who enshrined [[Ōkuninushi]] as Hyozu-no-Kami. Its original location, identified as {{nihongo|Yuzuki-dake|弓月岳|qq=}}, has been variously associated with Ryūōzan, Anashiyama, and Makimukuyama mountains. The shrine's primary deity, Hyozu-no-Kami, is currently enshrined in the central hall and is represented by a sacred mirror. The shrine identifies Hyozu-no-Kami with Miketsu-no-Kami, though other interpretations link the deity to [[Ame-no-Uzume]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], Tenfukino-Mikoto, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}}, or {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}. The former {{nihongo|Makimuku-ni-imasu Wakumusubi Shrine|巻向坐若御魂神社|qq=}} was located in Makimukuyama and enshrined Wakumusubi-no-Kami, now interpreted as Kushinada-hime and represented by magatama beads and bells. Some theories associate Wakumusubi-no-Kami with {{ill|Wakumusubi|ja|和久産巣日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakumusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワクムスビ}}. The oldest records of these shrines appear in the [[Shōsōin]] documents, which mention rituals performed in 730 CE. In 859 CE, Anashi-ni-imasu Hyōzu Shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}}, while Makimuku-ni-imasu Wakumusubi Shrine received {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. The origins of Anashi-no-Ōhyozu Shrine are unknown. Its deity, Ōhyozu-no-Kami, is enshrined in the left hall and is represented by a sacred sword. The deity has been identified as {{ill|Amenohiboko|ja|天日槍命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiboko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノヒボコ}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}, or Susanoo. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, the shrine’s main structures were destroyed during the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|qid=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War|1=Ōnin War}}. The remaining shrines were consolidated at the location of Anashi-no-Ōhyozu Shrine. In 1873, the shrine was designated a {{nihongo|gōsha|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=}}, and in 1928, it was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine. == Subordinate Shrines == A subsidiary shrine on the premises honors {{nihongo|[[Nomi no Sukune]]|野見宿禰|qq=}}, revered as the ancestor of sumo wrestling. == Access == * A 30-minute walk east from {{ill|Makimuku Station|en|Makimuku Station|qid=Q4980794|lt=Makimuku Station|1=Makimuku Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{nihongo|Sakurai Line|桜井線|qq=}}. ==See also== * [[Hyōzu Shrine]] * {{ill|Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|qq=}} * [[Itatehyōzu Shrine]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakurai.lg.jp/kanko/syaji/kashiramoji/agyo/1395291314493.html Anashi-ni-imasu Hyōzu Shrine] - Sakurai Tourism Information (in Japanese) {{Shinto shrines|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|version=100344853|comment=Imported full ja history}}
<!--Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine-->
<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Anashi-ni-imasu Hyōzu Shrine}}
== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]
'''Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine (Q11597229)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].
== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Shikijō district|ja|式上郡|zh|式上郡|qid=Q11486985|1=Shikijō district}} || {{ill|Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|lt=Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|commons|Category:Anashi-ni-imasu Hyōzu Shrine|qid=Q11597229|1=Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha|qid=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei|comment=categorized|1=Kanpei-sha}} ({{ill|Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings|qid=Q135009157|lt=Ainame|1=Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings}}{{efn|gets offerings for the {{ill|Ainame Festival|ja|相嘗祭|zh|相嘗祭|qid=Q11581944|1=Ainame Festival}} and the lower ranked {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|simple|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|qid=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭|qid=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}.}}) || {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} || — || — || {{coord|34.545051|135.858031|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=180779 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11597229}} [[da:d:Q11597229]] [[ja:穴師坐兵主神社]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]]==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:山辺の道]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sakurai]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]]
